bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name these authors who contributed to developing the notion of the "Great American Novel" in the 19th century:
parts: Many critics cite a novel about a raft trip down the Mississippi by this author from Hannibal, Missouri as the first Great American Novel.

This author introduced the abbreviation "GAN" for the Great American Novel. A park in Manhattan titles this author's novel about the heiress Catherine Sloper, who eventually rejects the gold-digging Morris Townsend.
This author of the Civil War novel Miss Ravenel's Conversion from Secession to Loyalty coined the term "Great American Novel" in an essay for The Nation magazine.
answers_sanitized: Henry James [prompt on James] (The novel is Washington Square.)
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: For 10 points each, name these authors who contributed to developing the notion of the "Great American Novel" in the 19th century:
parts_sanitized: Many critics cite a novel about a raft trip down the Mississippi by this author from Hannibal, Missouri as the first Great American Novel.

This author introduced the abbreviation "GAN" for the Great American Novel. A park in Manhattan titles this author's novel about the heiress Catherine Sloper, who eventually rejects the gold-digging Morris Townsend.
This author of the Civil War novel Miss Ravenel's Conversion from Secession to Loyalty coined the term "Great American Novel" in an essay for The Nation magazine.

bonuses.leadin: This author of The Golden Bowl created a novel where a governess thinks she is haunted by two orphaned children. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this late 19th century American author of The Turn of the Screw and The Ambassadors.
In this James novel, Catherine Sloper lives in a eponymous New York area with her aunt and father. The father tries to prevent Catherine from marrying partially because he lost his own wife.
In this perhaps more famous James novel, Isabel Archer falls for Gilbert Osmond. Osmond turns out to not be an ideal husband because he is having an affair with Madame Merle.

In this James novel, Catherine Sloper lives in a eponymous New York area with her aunt and father. The father tries to prevent Catherine from marrying partially because he lost his own wife.
In this perhaps more famous James novel, Isabel Archer falls for Gilbert Osmond. Osmond turns out to not be an ideal husband because he is having an affair with Madame Merle.

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, Isabel meets her disabled uncle, Ralph Touchett, after the death of her father. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which Isabel Archer rejects the marriage proposals of Lord Warburton and Casper Goodwood, and discovers that her husband Gilbert had an affair with Madame Merle.
This early modernist author of Portrait of a Lady wrote a novella about a governess who believes she sees the ghost of Miss Jessel, The Turn of the Screw.
In this novel by Henry James, Catherine Sloper is forced to choose between her love for Morris Townsend and her devotion to her father, Dr. Austin Sloper.
